manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: lackluster, renegotiation, impartiality, obliviousness, value, hesitance, reinstatement, impassion, unworthiness.
Your opponent's team words are: unsettlement, changelessness, direness, intermix, methodology, semantics, idiosyncrasy, insubordination.
Distractor words are: atonement, curiosity, avoidance, attunement, potentiality, misapprehension, panache.
The kill words are: subjection.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue related to some of our team's words, not mention any others, avoid kill word. Provide clue and target list. Choose a clue that ties multiple of ours. Our words: lackluster, renegotiation, impartiality, obliv obliviousness, value, hesitance, reinstatement, impassion, unworthiness.

Potential clue: "self" maybe for unworthiness, impassion? Could be "worth". But "worth" is part of value. Can't use part of the word. "Motivation" could tie impassion, value, hesitance? Not sure.

Perhaps "reassessment" ties renegotiation, reinstatement, value, impassion? Hmm.

Goal is to give as many as possible but ensure clue semantically connects. Could pick "confidence" relates to hesitance (lack), unworthiness, impassion, value. Might be ambiguous.

Better clue: "recovery" relates to reinstatement, impassion, value? Not clear.

Maybe we give separate turns. Provide one clue for two words: "bias" relates to impartiality (opposite) maybe not.

Better to choose clue "fairness" -> impartiality. Only one word. But could also connect to value? maybe not.

We need single clue. Let's aim for "renewal" linking reinstatement and impassion? Could be. Also lackluster maybe not.

Maybe give "hesitation" clue but cannot use part of target word? Hesitance vs hesitation, similar but maybe considered part? Might be borderline.

We could be safe: clue "justice" -> impartiality, unworthiness? hmm.

We just need to give any clue that relates to some of our team's words. Could be just one. So let's pick "fairness" => impartiality. Provide target list: impartiality.

Thus:

CLUE: fairness
TARGETS: impartiality

Make sure not to include other text
